# Import necessary libraries
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, IntegerType, StringType

# Define your storage details
storage_account_name = "ecomsalessa"
container_name = "raw"
storage_path = f"abfss://{container_name}@{storage_account_name}.dfs.core.windows.net/test_data/"

# 🔹 1. Create a sample DataFrame
schema = StructType([
    StructField("id", IntegerType(), True),
    StructField("name", StringType(), True),
    StructField("age", IntegerType(), True)
])

data = [
    (1, "Alice", 25),
    (2, "Bob", 30),
    (3, "Charlie", 22)
]

df = spark.createDataFrame(data, schema)
print("✅ DataFrame Created:")
df.show()

# 🔹 2. Write DataFrame to ADLS Gen2 in Parquet format
df.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(storage_path)
print(f"✅ Data written successfully to: {storage_path}")

# 🔹 3. Read Data Back from ADLS Gen2
df_read = spark.read.parquet(storage_path)
print("✅ Data Read from ADLS:")
df_read.show()

# Define the path of the test data
test_data_path = "abfss://raw@ecomsalessa.dfs.core.windows.net/test_data/"

# Use PySpark to delete the folder
spark._jvm.org.apache.hadoop.fs.FileSystem.get(
    spark._jsc.hadoopConfiguration()
).delete(
    spark._jvm.org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path(test_data_path), True
)

print(f"✅ Successfully deleted: {test_data_path}")
